name: Parque Natural Señorío de Bertiz – circular hasta el Palacio de Aizkolegi
region: Bertizko Jaurerria Natur Parkea / Parque Natural Señorío de Bértiz
region_intro: Hiking around Bertizko Jaurerria Natur Parkea / Parque Natural Señorío de Bértiz is characterized by its well-preserved Atlantic woodlands, featuring dense forests of beech, oak, and chestnut trees. The Bidasoa River flows through the park, adding to the scenic landscape. Topographically, the park includes 17 named mountains, with Aizkolegi being the highest peak at 841 meters above sea level. A unique feature is the Historic-Artistic Garden, which houses a collection of native and exotic tree species. Best hiking trails around Bertizko Jaurerria Natur Parkea / Parque Natural Señorío de Bértiz The most popular hiking route is Parque Natural de Bertiz – circular from Oronoz-Mugairi via Aizkolegi path, a 5.5 miles (8.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 31 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the park's natural features. Another top favourite among local hikers is Charcoal kilns – Bertiz Natural Park loop from Bertizarana, a moderate 7.5 miles (12.1 km) path. This trail winds through the park's woodlands, passing historical charcoal kilns. Local hikers also love the Lime Kiln – Charcoal kilns loop from Bertizarana, a 14.3 miles (22.9 km) trail leading through dense forests and past historical structures, often completed in about 6 hours 47 minutes. Hiking around Bertizko Jaurerria Natur Parkea / Parque Natural Señorío de Bértiz is defined by ancient Atlantic woodlands, the Bidasoa River valley, and ascents to peaks like Aizkolegi. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging treks. The routes in Bertizko Jaurerria Natur Parkea / Parque Natural Señorío de Bértiz are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from nearly 600 reviews. More than 4000 hikers have used komoot to explore Bertizko Jaurerria Natur Parkea / Parque Natural Señorío de Bértiz's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there in Bertizko Jaurerria Natur Parkea / Parque Natural Señorío de Bértiz?
A: The Bertizko Jaurerria Natur Parkea / Parque Natural Señorío de Bértiz offers nearly 200 hiking trails. This extensive network provides options for various ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging treks through its diverse landscapes.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Bertizko Jaurerria Natur Parkea?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from nearly 600 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the well-preserved Atlantic woodlands, the serene atmosphere, and the variety of paths that lead to historical sites and scenic viewpoints.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly hikes in the park?
A: Yes, the park offers 28 easy routes suitable for families. The Historic-Artistic Garden provides a serene walking experience with its diverse botanical collection, ponds, and careful ornamentation, perfect for a leisurely stroll. Many of the park's trails are well-maintained and offer picnic areas and children's play areas, making them ideal for family outings.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Bertizko Jaurerria Natur Parkea?
A: Yes, many of the park's trails can be combined to create beautiful circular routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e968771800/parque-natural-de-bertiz-circular-from-oronoz-mugairi-via-aizkolegi-path" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural de Bertiz – circular from Oronoz-Mugairi via Aizkolegi path&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate option that showcases the park's natural features. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10518091" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charcoal kilns – Bertiz Natural Park loop from Bertizarana&lt;/a&gt;, which winds through woodlands and past historical charcoal kilns.

Q: What notable landmarks or natural features can I see along the hiking trails?
A: Hikers can explore several notable landmarks and natural features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3013097" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aizkolegi Palace Ruins&lt;/a&gt;, a modernist hilltop mansion, offers breathtaking panoramic views. You can also discover the tranquil Bidasoa River, ancient Atlantic woodlands of beech and oak, and the unique Historic-Artistic Garden. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5321807" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aiskolegi Trail&lt;/a&gt; itself is a highlight, leading to the highest peak in the park.

Q: Are there any historical sites to explore while hiking?
A: Absolutely. The park is rich in history. You can hike to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3013097" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aizkolegi Palace Ruins&lt;/a&gt;, a significant historical landmark. Additionally, trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15007222" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lime Kiln – Charcoal kilns loop from Bertizarana&lt;/a&gt; pass by old charcoal platforms and lime kilns, offering a glimpse into the region's past as a managed forest.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Bertizko Jaurerria Natur Parkea?
A: The park's temperate and rainy climate, with few winter frosts, makes it suitable for hiking year-round. However, spring and autumn are particularly beautiful, with lush greenery and vibrant fall colors in the Atlantic woodlands. Summer offers pleasant conditions for exploring, while winter provides a unique, tranquil experience amidst the ancient forests.

Q: Is there parking available for hikers?
A: Yes, the park provides facilities for visitors, including parking areas. The Nature Interpretation Center, located at the park's entrance, is a common starting point and offers parking. Specific trailheads may also have designated parking spots.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Bertizko Jaurerria Natur Parkea?
A: While the region is generally welcoming to outdoor enthusiasts, it's always best to check specific park regulations regarding dogs, especially in protected natural areas. Typically, dogs are allowed on trails but often require to be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the comfort of other visitors.

Q: What kind of terrain and elevation can I expect on the trails?
A: The park's terrain varies from gentle paths through the Historic-Artistic Garden to more challenging ascents. It features magnificent Atlantic woodlands, often carpeted with ferns, and includes 17 named mountains. The highest peak, Aizkolegi, stands at 841 meters, offering routes with significant elevation gain and rewarding views, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31925925" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aizkolegi (841 m) – Aizkolegi Abandoned House loop from Baztan-Bidasoa&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any viewpoints with panoramic views?
A: Yes, the ascent to Aizkolegi, the highest peak at 841 meters, rewards hikers with breathtaking panoramic views, particularly towards the western Pyrenees. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3013097" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aizkolegi Palace Ruins&lt;/a&gt;, located at this elevation, is a prime spot for enjoying these expansive vistas.
tips:
The ruins of the Aizkolegi Palace are one of the most visited sites within the Señorío de Bertiz Natural Park.
Located on the homonymous summit at 841 meters above sea level, this wonderful place carries a legend that revolves around its former owners and founders. It is said that Pedro de Ziga and Doña Dorotea Fernández built the palace and were also the ones who created the botanical garden in 1892 with species brought from other countries such as beech, Chinese palm, lime or bamboo. According to the story, it was Doña Dorotea who chose this place since from here she could see Biarritz, which was the place of residence of her lover.
This is one of the infinite paths and paths that are found within the Señorío de Bertiz park, a wonderful place that is worth visiting.
